ground_truth.title: New and Upcoming Books, Seasonal Book Listings | Publishers Weekly
ground_truth.main_content:
Adult Previews
Books for At-Home Entertaining
Authors roll out the welcome mat with new books on hosting, gathering, and party planning more...
Cocktail Books for All Types of Drinkers
Low- and no-booze options cater to the health-conscious and sober-curious. more...
Mysteries & Thrillers in Translation
Authors speak the universal language of crime fiction. more...
Adult Books for Spring 2026
By David Adams
Our editors have sifted through thousands of submissions to bring you the season’s most notable new releases, from game-changing histories to high-voltage romantasies. more...
Personal Finance Books for Today’s Economy
By Liz Scheier
New titles help readers navigate a changing financial picture. more...
Recently Posted:
Spring 2026 Fiction & Nonfiction Preview: SF, Fantasy & Horror
By Phoebe Cramer | 12/05/2025 @ 07:00PM
Fairy-tale elements dominate this season’s fantasies, gothic atmosphere pervades the horror novels, and tech-based anxiety animates the sci-fi outings.
Spring 2026 Fiction & Nonfiction Preview: Science
By Marisa Charpentier | 12/05/2025 @ 07:00PM
Space exploration, debates over AI, and stories of wildlife resilience headline this season’s science titles.
Spring 2026 Fiction & Nonfiction Preview: Romance & Erotica
By Phoebe Cramer | 12/05/2025 @ 07:00PM
This season’s offerings balance the light—from Regencies to rom-coms—with the dark, including angsty romantasies and high-heat horror.
Spring 2026 Fiction & Nonfiction Preview: Politics & Current Events
By Dana Snitzky | 12/05/2025 @ 07:00PM
Government malfeasance, the consequences of too much tech, and the emotional lives of medical caregivers are spotlighted in spring’s current affairs titles.
Spring 2026 Fiction & Nonfiction Preview: Poetry
By Maya Popa | 12/05/2025 @ 07:00PM
Alongside collections confronting political upheaval and technological acceleration are works that celebrate place, art, and the enduring power of literature.
Spring 2026 Fiction & Nonfiction Preview: Mysteries & Thrillers
by Conner Reed | 12/05/2025 @ 07:00PM
On tap this season: promising debuts, provocative prequels, and new novels from Jessica Knoll and Jo Nesbø.
Spring 2026 Fiction & Nonfiction Preview: Memoirs & Biographies
by Conner Reed | 12/05/2025 @ 07:00PM
This season features self-portraits from pop culture personalities including Liza Minnelli and Lena Dunham, plus biographies of literary luminaries.
Spring 2026 Fiction & Nonfiction Preview: Literary Fiction
By David Varno | 12/05/2025 @ 07:00PM
In spring’s most anticipated novels, the bonds of families and friends are stretched to the breaking point and the past rears its ugly head.
Spring 2026 Fiction & Nonfiction Preview: Lifestyle
By Miriam Grossman | 12/05/2025 @ 07:00PM
Spring brings explorations of the complexities of women’s healthcare, social connections in the digital age, and the search for purpose in a volatile world.
Spring 2026 Fiction & Nonfiction Preview: History
By Dana Snitzky | 12/05/2025 @ 07:00PM
This season, culture wars and their origins are dissected, fascism and its opponents are profiled, and enclaves of the rich and famous are infiltrated.
